Summary: Phase 1 render service (Puppeteer image render)
Description: Spin up a render endpoint that takes { mediaId, template, aspectRatio, format } and returns a rendered PNG. Uses Puppeteer to load a stripped-down /render.html page that reuses the preview render code at exact pixel dimensions, screenshots, uploads to Cloudinary, persists RenderArtifact. Image-only for Phase 1; video comes in Phase 2.

Summary: Inline Render button on each Creative Template Preview card
Description: Add a Render PNG action next to the validation status on each preview card in the Creative Template Preview panel. On click: POST /api/render, show spinner, display thumbnail + download link when done.

Summary: Rendered Creatives gallery panel
Description: New section below Creative Template Preview showing all RenderArtifacts for the current Media. Each card: template name, aspect ratio, format, thumbnail, file size, Open / Download / Push-to-campaign actions. Includes a Render all button that queues every template-ratio combo that passes validation.

Summary: Batch render on rights approval
Description: When rights.approved flips to true, enqueue render jobs for the top N viable (template, ratio) combinations so creatives are pre-warmed for campaign push.

Summary: Phase 2 render service: separate deployment + video rendering
Description: Split the render service into its own Render deployment with a Puppeteer worker pool. Add ffmpeg-based video pipeline for MP4 output; compose source video clips with overlays via CSS animation captured as frames. Migrate off in-process deployment.

Summary: Video overlay zones: pass cross-frame safeRect as forbidden to Gemini
Description: The detect pipeline already computes safeRect (union of all YOLO detections across frames + primary subjects). Thread it into the overlay-zone Gemini prompt as an explicit forbidden rect so creator-subject motion doesn't collide with overlays in the clip.

Summary: Video overlay zones: multi-frame analysis (sample 3 frames)
Description: For video Media, sample start/middle/end frames of the clip, run overlay-zone analysis on each, union the forbidden rects and intersect the safe zones. Replaces the current hero-frame-only analysis.

Summary: Video overlay zones: analyze rendered self-underlay output
Description: Extract N frames from the rendered self-underlay Cloudinary video URL via so_<sec> transforms and analyze those instead of the hero frame. Highest fidelity, highest cost; only do this once real overlay-on-video failures surface.

Summary: Populate media.supporting_media with secondary smart-crop winner
Description: Currently null; slot chain falls through to product.hero_media so results_proof shows the same image twice. Pick an alternate-ratio smart-crop winner as media.supporting_media[0] so the support_media zone renders a complementary framing.

Summary: Populate media.background_media as source fallback
Description: Populate media.background_media with the source fileUrl so templates that reach the end of the support_media source_priority chain have a blurred-bg fallback asset.

Summary: Add overlay-mode variants for other templates
Description: Roll out the image-as-canvas placement pattern to ugc_split_screen, review_collage, results_proof, creator_endorsement — register each as <template>_overlay with its own priority + region hints. Validate against real media.

Summary: Iterate overlay placement quality from real-world renders
Description: Review placement outputs across brands/ratios, tune region hints, size bounds, scrim strength, inset fallback thresholds based on visual quality feedback.

Summary: Add 16:9 aspect ratio to extendedCropsService
Description: The renderer's canvas spec supports 16:9 but extendedCropsService only produces 9:16 and 1.91:1. Add 16:9 entry to NEW_RATIOS so horizontal placements get AI-extended assets instead of falling back to a base-ratio winner.

Summary: Judge bias fix: defects-list pass before scoring
Description: The current extended-crop judge has residual self-preference bias for gpt-image-1 over Gemini that slot-blinding + weighting + tighter rejects didn't fully address. Change the prompt to require an itemized per-candidate defects list before scoring. Forces detection of label / shape / text mutations a holistic score glosses over.

Summary: Judge bias fix: cross-provider second opinion
Description: Run Gemini 2.5 Pro as a second judge in parallel with the GPT-4.1 judge; only declare a winner when both models agree. On disagreement, fall back to the label_logo_fidelity leader. Structurally eliminates self-preference.

Summary: Judge bias fix: OCR verification of source text
Description: We already detect source text in subjectTextService. Run OCR on each extended-crop candidate and auto-reject any that's missing a detected source token. Objective tie-break.

Summary: Remove Gemini-extension winner override
Description: Currently the judge's pick is overridden to force Gemini extension as winner due to unresolved bias. Remove this stopgap once one of the judge-bias fixes (defects-list / cross-provider / OCR) lands and the organic winner is trustworthy.

Summary: Feed YOLO detections into GPT subject-text prompt as anchors
Description: Currently YOLO and GPT subject-text run independently. Pass YOLO bboxes into the GPT prompt as known objects so the two streams reconcile at detection time instead of disagreeing at judge time. Improves primarySubject quality.

Summary: Reviews via follow-up SerpAPI endpoint
Description: Currently reviews come from the Gemini-synthesized summary (notional/derived). SerpAPI's google_product engine is retired but the google_shopping response includes reviews_results.serpapi_endpoint which points to a follow-up reviews call — chain it to get real individual review rows.

Summary: Use smart-crop winner URL for Google Lens matching
Description: productMatchService passes the raw source imageUrl to Lens — for UGC this usually frames the person. Swap to the 1:1 smart-crop judge winner URL (subject-tight crop) for cleaner Lens matches.

Summary: Upload YOLO primary product crop for tightest Lens match
Description: For video Media, upload the YOLO primary product cropBuffer as a separate Cloudinary image and pass its URL to Lens. Tightest possible match isolation.

Summary: Brand curation UI (list + edit)
Description: Simple admin screen at /brands that lists all Brand docs (stub / enriched / curated), supports edit of name, tagline, logo, colors, font, demographics. Flips source=curated so automatic enrichment never overwrites. Needed once stub-only brands are obviously wrong.

Summary: Persona avatar generation via gpt-image-1
Description: Generate an avatarUrl per demographic in Brand.demographics[] by calling gpt-image-1 with the persona description. Uploads to Cloudinary, sets demographic.avatarUrl. Used by the renderer for quote attribution avatars in creator_endorsement / review_collage.

Summary: Brand font loading in preview + renderer
Description: When brand.font_family is set, load the font from Google Fonts or a bundled WOFF/WOFF2 in the preview page and the Puppeteer render context. Currently we just set the CSS font-family and hope the viewer has it installed.

Summary: BrandCanvasVariant promotion flow from AI Layout Studio
Description: If AI Layout Studio outputs prove viable, add a Use this layout button on each extracted reference that promotes it to a BrandCanvasVariant document (brandId, variantName, aspectRatio, canvasSpec). Template picker surfaces these alongside the 5 canonical templates.

Summary: Webhook ingestion for Meta / TikTok / Instagram
Description: Receive posts via webhook, upsert Media by (source, externalId), populate metadata.{creatorName, creatorHandle, caption, postedAt, hashtags}, platformStats.{views, likes, comments, shares, saves}, rights state. Enqueue DetectRun automatically. Unblocks real engagement data for performance.metrics and performance.engagement.

Summary: adSuitability scoring writer
Description: Compute Media.adSuitability { score, reasons, updatedAt } after a DetectRun completes. Heuristic over product count, primary-subject quality, judge scores, engagement density, review signal. Powers a Find ad-suitable media query for future ad-layout service batches.

Summary: Dedicated rights management screen
Description: Standalone screen listing all Media with pending / approved / revoked rights, batch approval, audit of approvedBy / approvedAt / notes. Current inline toggle on detect review page is adequate for single-media flow but won't scale once webhook ingestion feeds many media at once.

Summary: Product records with source lineage on approval
Description: When an operator approves a YOLO product card into inventory, the resulting Product doc should carry sourceMediaId, sourceRunId, sourceYoloProductId so we can audit where it came from and re-run with the original media if needed.

Summary: Migrate legacy inventory pipeline to Run-style entity
Description: The truck-photo upload and pre-cropped bridge still use the legacy Job model. Move to a PrecroppedRun / InventoryRun lifecycle consistent with DetectRun so both paths share the split-service architecture pattern.

Summary: Consume overlay restrictions + brightnessGrid in renderer
Description: The renderer v1 doc references scrim application rules but doesn't reference our per-image restrictions[] / brightnessGrid. Wire the overlay-zone analysis into the renderer so scrim placement and hard keep-outs are driven by real image data rather than hardcoded heuristics.

Summary: Expose judge primaryId as the single source of truth
Description: resolvePrimarySubjectDesc already prefers judge.subjects.primaryId. Audit all other consumers of subjects (extended crops prompts, match service, overlay zones) to make sure none of them silently fall back to GPT's role assignment when a judge pick exists. Document the precedence rule in the subject-text service header.

Summary: Inset-mode band crowding: CTA fails to place after headline consumes full band
Description: In inset mode, higher-priority elements (logo, headline, product_meta) consume the full width of a narrow band, leaving no room for the CTA. Candidate generator in inset path emits left/centered/right anchors per shape but not anchors that sit RIGHT-OF-CONSUMED. Smoke test: 9:16 portrait with centered-person subject reliably reproduces. Fix: sub-divide bands by consumed rects between elements, or add a right-of-consumed anchor, or shrink earlier elements more aggressively when a required later element would otherwise fail.

Summary: Enforce canvas safe-margin in placement
Description: Today candidate generation uses scattered 0.04/0.05/0.95/0.96 literals so effective safe margins drift between 2%-5% depending on region (bottom-third anchor at y=0.90 + shape h=0.08 lands y2=0.98 = 2% from edge). Step 1: introduce SAFE_MARGIN const (0.05) and withinSafeArea helper; replace the scattered literals. Step 2 (later): per-edge margins keyed on platform (Meta/TikTok/IG reserve different HUD/caption zones).

Summary: Wire orphan canonical fields into templates
Description: Audit found ~15 canonical input fields that are derived but never rendered: theme.{style,background_style,emphasis}, layout_options.*, copy.{highlight_text,disclaimer}, cta.{subtext,offer_text}, product.{description,short_benefits,category,currency,id}, trust.{retailer_logos,certifications,press_mentions}, performance.engagement.{shares,saves,views}, ugc.{post_id,post_type,shares,saves,rights_approved}, creator.avatar. Decide per-field: kill, expose in canonical templates, or wire into overlay-mode renderer. Especially high-leverage: theme block (could drive emphasis-based priority reshuffling, style overrides, background treatments, variant generation) and copy.eyebrow (small kicker above overlay headline).

Summary: Hybrid art-director: LLM theme/treatment decisions, heuristic placement execution
Description: Use one Gemini Vision call per Media to emit high-level art direction (theme.emphasis, theme.style, theme.background_style, per-element treatment hints) — then heuristic placement engine executes those decisions across every (template, aspect_ratio) combo. Trades off pure-LLM placement (slow, costly, non-deterministic, weak coordinate math, no measure-and-shrink) for LLM aesthetic judgment + heuristic exactness. Cost collapses from O(templates × ratios) to O(1) calls/Media; outputs stay deterministic and debuggable; brand voice stays consistent across ratios. Implementation order: (1) emit theme block during layout-input assembly, (2) wire theme.emphasis to placement-priority shuffle, (3) wire theme.style to typography overrides table, (4) wire theme.background_style to Cloudinary bg treatment.

Summary: detect.html: live-react to brand-picker change
Description: ads.html listens for window brand:change and reloads its media library. detect.html does not — page-load picks up the new X-Brand-Id but a brand switch while on detect leaves stale data visible. Add a brand:change listener that clears state + redraws.

Summary: detect upload form: drop redundant Brand-name field
Description: With the nav brand picker active, the brand the user is uploading for is implicit (X-Brand-Id header). The Brand input on the upload form is now duplicate context and a source of confusion (what if user types a different brand than the one selected?). Replace the input with a small Active brand: <name> indicator + a Change link that opens the picker.

Summary: Brand management UI (rename / archive / delete)
Description: Today brands are immutable from the UI — only created via the picker. Need a settings page or modal to: rename a brand, archive (soft-delete with status), permanently delete (with confirmation + media cascade rules). Also: edit websiteUrl, primaryColor, custom logoUrl, tone[], demographics. Today the catalog mutates only via auto-enrichment + curatedFields lock; this surfaces those fields directly.

Summary: Tenancy phase 4: team invitations + multi-Advertiser membership
Description: Add AdvertiserMembership join collection so a user can belong to multiple Advertisers (agency model). Invitation flow: an admin emails an invite link with a signed token, the invitee accepts to join the Advertiser as editor/viewer. requireAuth gains an active-Advertiser concept (header X-Advertiser-Id similar to X-Brand-Id) and falls back to the users sole membership when not specified.

Summary: IG V2: CLIP visual matching of YOLO crops against catalog images
Description: Generate CLIP embeddings on catalog Product images at ingest, store as Float32 vector on Product. Match service: encode each YOLO crop and rank catalog Products by cosine similarity. Catalog visual hits with cosine > threshold supplement (or replace) text+category matching. This is the real unlock for "model wearing what we sell" matches where text overlap is weak (a generic "tee shirt" YOLO label binds to the right SKU because its visual signature matches one catalog item).

Summary: IG V2: scheduled auto-sync with per-brand daily DetectRun cap
Description: Cron job pulls catalog daily and IG posts hourly per connected Brand. Cap auto-DetectRuns at N posts/day per brand to control compute spend. Manual sync buttons remain. Add brand-level setting for cadence + cap. Skip already-ingested externalIds via the upsert key.

Summary: IG V2: catalog browser panel on brand.html
Description: Paginated grid of Product rows under each Brand: image thumbnail, title, price, productUrl, last-seen-in-match count. Filters by category, in-stock, has-reviews. "Refresh reviews" button per Product. Lets ops eyeball whether catalog sync produced sensible data and which SKUs are seeing match traffic.

Summary: IG V2: media-source indicator on detect.html
Description: Distinguish uploaded vs IG-sourced Media in the inventory list and detect view. Small pill: "Manual upload" / "Instagram post" / "Instagram reel". For IG-sourced, surface the original IG URL, posted date, and creator handle as read-only metadata.

Summary: IG V2: multi-page-per-brand + Reels-specific frame extraction
Description: Allow more than one IG account per Brand (e.g. main brand page + creator collabs). Reels need per-frame YOLO + audio Whisper transcription; static posts skip transcription. Auto-detect media type from Graph API media_type field and route to the right pipeline shape.

Summary: IG V3: Meta webhooks for real-time post ingest
Description: Subscribe to Meta page-feed webhooks. New post events trigger immediate ingest + DetectRun (subject to daily cap). Requires Meta app review for webhook subscriptions in production. Eliminates polling latency.

Summary: IG V3: cross-brand catalog dedup
Description: Same SKU sold under multiple Advertiser accounts (agencies, parent/child brands) creates duplicate Product rows. Add an optional canonicalProductId pointer or a (gtin, brandFamily) dedup index. Reviews + match-history can then be shared across the duplicates.

Summary: IG V3: auto-suggest product matches in IG comments
Description: When a post triggers a confident product_match outcome, optionally post a reply comment on the original IG post linking to the matched product page. Requires explicit per-Brand opt-in and rate limiting. Closes the loop from social signal to commerce conversion.

Summary: Catalog browser: surface review quotes when hasReviews=true
Description: The /api/integrations/instagram/catalog list endpoint returns hasReviews, reviewsRating, and reviewsCount but omits the actual quotes blob to keep paginated responses light. Decide between two surfacings: (a) return productReviews inline in the list response when hasReviews=true (heavier payload), or (b) add an expand-on-click flow in the card that fetches GET /catalog/:id/reviews on demand. Recommend (b) — keeps the grid fast and lets the card animate in 4-6 quotes inline. Either way the card should show at least one teaser quote without a round-trip.

Summary: Ad platforms: end-to-end OAuth + scope validation
Description: Cap off Ad Platforms Phase A by validating both Meta Ads and Google Ads OAuth flows against real ad accounts. Punchlist: (1) Meta App — request Advanced Access on ads_read / ads_management / business_management via App Review (Standard Access works for the app's own admin/dev/tester roles, blocks public users); (2) Meta App — confirm both /instagram/callback and /meta-ads/callback are registered as Valid OAuth Redirect URIs; (3) Google Cloud — get the OAuth consent screen verified for the sensitive adwords scope, or list our admin emails as Test users while in Testing publishing status; (4) Google Ads API Center — confirm developer token has Standard or Basic Access (not just Test); (5) e2e walk: connect each platform from the brand page, finalize via picker, sync campaigns once, verify Campaign rows land with productSetIds populated. Tracks the lingering Meta OAuth perms questions + the Google v17 sunset that surfaced during testing.

Summary: Detect pipeline: within-run parallelism (Promise.all stage groups)
Description: Today pipelines/detect.js runs stages strictly sequentially even when there are no data dependencies between them. Identify groups that can run in parallel and wrap each group in a single Promise.all. First-pass candidates with verified independence: (a) post-detection enrichment fan-out — subjects-text (GPT-4.1) + product-match providers + transcript NER all read the YOLO output but write to separate artifacts, can run as one Promise.all instead of three serial awaits; (b) smart-crops + extended-crops can start the moment detection completes, in parallel with subjects-text; (c) overlay-zones analysis only needs hero crop + safeRect — can fan out per ratio. Expected 30-50%% speedup per run on top of the WORKER_CONCURRENCY parallelism we already have across runs. Risks: stage timings telemetry needs reworking (currently single-threaded sequential timing), and error handling has to fail the whole run if any parallel branch throws (use Promise.allSettled + collect errors instead of bubble). Worth a careful PR — affects every run.
column9: Currently any Google account can sign in (allowlist removed in 540f01e to unblock dev). Before public launch, add a gate: invite tokens (similar to AdvertiserMembership invitations already shipped), or an email-domain allowlist on Advertiser, or a waitlist flow that holds new signups in a 'pending' status until an admin approves. Whichever we pick, the check goes back into the GoogleStrategy callback in server/index.js where the @floodqrf.com check used to live.

Summary: IG V3 followup: UGC rights-request comment flow
Description: Distinct from V3 #3 (commerce nudges on the brand's own posts). When a creator's post features the brand (discovered via /me/tags or branded-content tags), auto-comment asking for permission to use the content in marketing. Components: (1) discovery — periodically pull tagged-in posts via Graph API endpoint; (2) per-brand opt-in + custom template referencing creator handle and a terms URL; (3) reply parser — when the creator replies with the configured magic word (default "YES"), flip the Media's rights.approved=true and stamp the consent message as audit trail; (4) cap + already-asked idempotency on Media.metadata.rightsRequestAt. Requires the same instagram_manage_comments scope already added in V3 #3 plus instagram_basic for tagged-media reads.

Summary: Ad Platforms Phase B — Campaign read sync
Description: Read campaigns + ad sets/groups + ads from connected Meta and Google Ads accounts. Capture targeting (demo, geo, interests), schedule (start/end), objective, status, budget, and the product set / merchant center IDs each ad references — those product IDs are the join key that lets ads.html filter media-picker results to ones that match the active campaign's products. Read-only in this epic; ship-to-platform (Phase D) is separate. Stories below cover schema + Meta sync + Google sync + scheduler + UI; written so they can be done in parallel after the schema lands.

Summary: Phase B-1: Campaign collection + per-platform sync interface
Description: New Campaign mongoose model { advertiserId, brandId, platform: 'meta-ads'|'google-ads', externalId, name, status, objective, budget, schedule {start,end}, targeting {demo,geo,interests}, productSetIds[], adSets: [{ externalId, name, status, productSetId, ads: [{externalId, name, creativeRef}] }], lastSyncedAt }. Compound unique index (brandId, platform, externalId) for idempotent upsert. Plus a stub services/campaignSyncService.js with the platform-dispatch shape (calls metaAds vs googleAds adapters). Foundation for B-2 / B-3 / B-4.

Summary: Phase B-2: Meta Ads campaign sync adapter
Description: services/metaAdsCampaignService.js. Uses the stored long-lived token + ad-account id from the IntegrationCredential to paginate /act_<id>/campaigns?fields=id,name,status,objective,daily_budget,lifetime_budget,start_time,stop_time,promoted_object,product_set_id then per-campaign /campaign-id/adsets and /adset-id/ads. Maps Meta's targeting spec (geo_locations, age_min, age_max, interests) into the unified Campaign.targeting shape. Idempotent upsert keyed on (brandId, platform='meta-ads', externalId). Captures Meta product_set_id on each ad set so the matcher can filter Media by overlapping CatalogProducts later.

Summary: Phase B-3: Google Ads campaign sync adapter
Description: services/googleAdsCampaignService.js. Uses the stored refresh_token to mint an access token, then the searchStream endpoint with a GAQL query pulling campaign + ad_group + ad + ad_group_criterion + customer_negative_criterion. Pulls Performance Max asset groups + their listing_group_filter (which references Merchant Center product IDs) so Phase C can match those against CatalogProduct. Maps Google's targeting (geo, age, audience criteria) into the unified Campaign.targeting shape. Idempotent upsert keyed on (brandId, platform='google-ads', externalId).

Summary: Phase B-4: Sync routes + manual-trigger UI
Description: REST endpoints under /api/integrations/{meta-ads,google-ads}/sync-campaigns and /campaign-status. Mirror the IG catalog/posts route shape (foreground sync, returns counts + perCredential breakdown). Frontend: "Sync campaigns" button in each Ad Platforms card row plus an ingested-count pill + last-synced timestamp. Read-only for now — campaign list view is Phase B-6.

Summary: Phase B-5: Scheduled auto-sync of campaigns
Description: Extend services/scheduledSyncService.js with a campaigns tier: every Brand.syncSettings.campaignCadenceHours (default 6h) sweep active meta-ads + google-ads credentials and refresh their campaigns. Reuses the existing tick interval; per-credential lastCampaignSyncAt timestamp on IntegrationCredential. Brand-level toggle reuses existing autoSyncEnabled flag — no new opt-in switch needed.

Summary: Phase B-6: Campaign browser panel on brand page
Description: Collapsible card under Ad Platforms (mirrors the V2 #1 catalog browser). Paginated list of Campaigns showing platform pill, name, objective, status, schedule, ad-set count, and product-set overlap with our CatalogProduct collection. Filters by platform / status / objective. Per-row drill-down expands to show ad sets + ads + targeting summary. Read-only — drives the Phase C campaign-aware media filter on ads.html.
